description: Combined results from /design skill pipeline on 10 yeast synbio benchmark cases

heterologous_genes.function: Phenylalanine ammonia-lyase; converts L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamate (725 aa, Km=68 uM)
Trans-cinnamate 4-monooxygenase; hydroxylates cinnamate to 4-coumarate (505 aa, CYP450)
NADPH-cytochrome P450 reductase; electron donor for C4H, IFS, and I2'H CYP450s (711 aa, PDB:5GXU)
4-Coumarate-CoA ligase; activates coumarate with CoA to form p-coumaroyl-CoA (561 aa)
NAD(P)H-dependent 6'-deoxychalcone synthase; produces isoliquiritigenin from coumaroyl-CoA + malonyl-CoA (315 aa)
Chalcone isomerase; cyclizes isoliquiritigenin to liquiritigenin (222 aa)
2-Hydroxyisoflavanone synthase; converts liquiritigenin to trihydroxyisoflavanone with aryl migration (523 aa, Km=8 uM, CYP450)
2-Hydroxyisoflavanone dehydratase; dehydrates trihydroxyisoflavanone to daidzein (328 aa, Km=210 uM)
Isoflavone 2'-hydroxylase; hydroxylates daidzein at 2' position (499 aa, CYP450)
Isoflavone reductase; reduces 2'-hydroxydaidzein to dihydrodaidzein (318 aa, Km=14.2 uM, PDB:2GAS)
Pterocarpan synthase; dirigent protein catalyzing cyclization to form the isoflavan scaffold (188 aa, PDB:6OOC)

knockouts.rationale: Major pyruvate decarboxylase; knockout (with PDC5, PDC6, ARO10) eliminates ethanol fermentation, redirecting carbon flux toward biomass and product
PDC5 isozyme; must be knocked out with PDC1 to fully disable pyruvate decarboxylase due to OR-logic GPR
PDC6 isozyme; required for complete PYRDC inactivation
Broad-specificity decarboxylase for aromatic 2-oxo acids; knockout prevents Ehrlich pathway degradation of phenylpyruvate and completes PYRDC inactivation

overexpressions.rationale: First committed step of oxidative pentose phosphate pathway; overexpression increases NADPH supply; pathway requires 6 NADPH per glabridin molecule
Second NADPH-generating step in oxidative PPP; overexpression with ZWF1 maximizes NADPH regeneration capacity

fba_results.strategy_description: 10-step heterologous pathway from L-phenylalanine to glabridin was added to iMM904 (11 reactions including exchange). Baseline theoretical max was 0.667 mmol/gDW/h. Overexpression of ZWF1 (G6PDH2r) and GND2 (GND) at min_flux=1.5 maintained near-theoretical-max production (0.666 mmol/gDW/h) at 10% biomass constraint. PDC quadruple knockout (PDC1/5/6 + ARO10) disabled ethanol fermentation and Ehrlich pathway but reduced max product flux to 0.452 due to redox imbalance; recommended as optional for fed-batch conditions. Production envelope shows classic growth-production tradeoff with full production at low growth rates.

heterologous_genes.function: Delta(24)-sterol reductase; converts zymosterol to 5alpha-cholest-8-en-3beta-ol using NADPH. Redirects flux from ergosterol to cholesterol pathway.
7-dehydrocholesterol reductase; converts 7-dehydrocholesterol to cholesterol using NADPH. Final step of cholesterol biosynthesis.

Cholesterol side-chain cleavage enzyme (P450scc); converts cholesterol to pregnenolone via three sequential oxidations. Requires adrenodoxin (FDX1) and adrenodoxin reductase (FDXR) as electron transfer partners. PDB: 3MZS.
Adrenodoxin (ferredoxin-1); 2Fe-2S iron-sulfur protein that shuttles electrons from FDXR to CYP11A1 for cholesterol side-chain cleavage. 186 aa.
NADPH:adrenodoxin oxidoreductase; FAD-containing flavoprotein that transfers electrons from NADPH to adrenodoxin (FDX1) for mitochondrial P450 systems. PDB: 1CJC.

knockouts.rationale: Block the native ergosterol branch at zymosterol to redirect flux toward cholesterol via the heterologous DHCR24. ERG6 KO eliminates zymosterol methylation to fecosterol, the first committed step of the ergosterol-specific pathway. Cholesterol can partially substitute for ergosterol in yeast membranes.

overexpressions.rationale: Truncated HMG-CoA reductase (catalytic domain only) is feedback-resistant and increases mevalonate pathway flux, the rate-limiting step in sterol biosynthesis.
Overexpress squalene synthase to increase carbon flux from FPP into the sterol pathway and away from competing isoprenoid branches.
Overexpress farnesyl diphosphate synthase to increase FPP supply for squalene/sterol biosynthesis.
Overexpress the pentose phosphate pathway entry enzyme to increase NADPH supply. The pathway from zymosterol to pregnenolone consumes 6 NADPH per molecule.

fba_results.strategy_description: Heterologous pathway (DHCR24, EBP, DHCR7, CYP11A1/FDX1/FDXR) inserted from zymosterol to pregnenolone with exchange reaction. Overexpress ERG9 (squalene synthase, min_flux=0.05), ZWF1 (G6PDH, min_flux=1.0), ERG20 (FPP synthase), and tHMG1 (feedback-resistant HMG-CoA reductase). ERG6 knockout recommended to redirect zymosterol from ergosterol to cholesterol pathway. At 10% min biomass fraction, max pregnenolone flux = 0.1428 mmol/gDW/h (99.9% of theoretical max 0.1429). Production envelope shows favorable growth-production tradeoff with >0.14 flux maintained up to 40% max biomass.

cases.product: Short- and medium-chain fatty acids (C8, C10, C12)

heterologous_genes.function: Medium-chain acyl-ACP thioesterase with C8>C10 substrate preference; cleaves octanoyl-ACP and decanoyl-ACP to release free C8 and C10 fatty acids from the FAS elongation cycle
Lauroyl-ACP thioesterase with C12>C14 preference (Km 1.9 uM for C12-ACP); cleaves dodecanoyl-ACP to release free C12 fatty acid; has PDB structure 5X04
Cytoplasmic thioesterase I (leaderless variant); hydrolyzes acyl-CoA thioesters C10-C18 with broad specificity; Km 11.5 uM for decanoyl-CoA; multiple PDB structures available

knockouts.reaction_blocked: FAO80p, FAO141p, FAO161p, FAO181p, FAO182p, FAO240p, ACOAO4-9p (all peroxisomal beta-oxidation, 20 reactions disabled)
knockouts.rationale: POX1 encodes the sole acyl-CoA oxidase in yeast peroxisomes; knockout abolishes all peroxisomal beta-oxidation, preventing degradation of C8, C10, and C12 fatty acids back to acetyl-CoA

knockouts.reaction_blocked: FAS141, FAS161, FAS181 (unsaturated fatty acid elongation/desaturation)
knockouts.rationale: ELO1 elongates C12 to C14:1, C14 to C16:1, C16 to C18:1 unsaturated fatty acids; knockout prevents carbon diversion from the SMCFA pool into unsaturated long-chain fatty acids

overexpressions.reaction_boosted: ACCOAC (acetyl-CoA + ATP + HCO3- --> malonyl-CoA + ADP + Pi)
overexpressions.rationale: ACC1 catalyzes the rate-limiting step of fatty acid biosynthesis converting acetyl-CoA to malonyl-CoA; overexpression increases malonyl-CoA supply, the primary C2-donor for FA chain elongation; baseline flux 0.1145

ZWF1 encodes glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase, the entry point of the pentose phosphate pathway and the major cytoplasmic NADPH source; overexpression increases NADPH availability for FA biosynthesis (14 NADPH needed per C12 molecule)

overexpressions.reaction_boosted: ACS (acetate + ATP + CoA --> acetyl-CoA + AMP + PPi)
overexpressions.rationale: ACS catalyzes cytoplasmic acetyl-CoA production from acetate; overexpression increases the cytoplasmic acetyl-CoA pool, the starter unit for FA synthesis; baseline flux 0.1471

fba_results.strategy_description: Baseline theoretical max for octanoate (C8): 1.83 mmol/gDW/h at 0% biomass. With 10% biomass constraint: 1.65 mmol/gDW/h. Knockouts of POX1 (beta-oxidation, 20 rxns disabled) and ELO1 (unsaturated FA elongation, 3 rxns disabled) prevent product degradation and carbon diversion. Decanoate (C10) max: 1.44 (0% biomass), 1.30 (10% biomass). Dodecanoate (C12) max: 1.18 (0% biomass), 1.07 (10% biomass). Production envelope shows linear growth-production tradeoff. Key bottleneck is the ethanol pathway (PYRDC flux ~19.5, ALCD2ir ~12.6) competing for pyruvate-derived acetyl-CoA. Heterologous medium-chain thioesterases (CvFatB1 from C. viscosissima, UcFatB1 from U. californica, TesA' from E. coli) are essential to intercept FAS intermediates at C8/C10/C12 chain lengths rather than allowing full elongation to C16/C18.

heterologous_genes.function: Phenylalanine ammonia-lyase; converts L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamate + NH3; first committed step of phenylpropanoid pathway
Trans-cinnamate 4-monooxygenase (cytochrome P450 73); hydroxylates trans-cinnamate to 4-coumarate using NADPH and O2
NADPH-cytochrome P450 reductase; required electron transfer partner for CYP73A5 (C4H); uses FAD and FMN cofactors
4-Coumarate--CoA ligase; activates 4-coumarate to p-coumaroyl-CoA using ATP and CoA; Km=252 uM for 4-coumarate
Chalcone synthase; condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with 3 malonyl-CoA; works with CHR to produce isoliquiritigenin instead of naringenin chalcone
Chalcone reductase; co-acts with CHS to reduce 6'-position during chalcone formation, producing isoliquiritigenin (the deoxychalcone) using NADPH
Chalcone isomerase; cyclizes isoliquiritigenin to liquiritigenin; Km=15.7 uM for 6'-deoxychalcone; has crystal structures (PDB: 1EYP, 1EYQ)

knockouts.rationale: Blocks degradation of phenylpyruvate (from phenylalanine catabolism) to phenylacetaldehyde, preserving phenylalanine precursor pool for the heterologous pathway
Partial reduction of pyruvate decarboxylase activity to reduce ethanol overflow and redirect carbon flux toward acetyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA for the pathway
Further reduction of pyruvate decarboxylase activity; combined with PDC5 KO, leaves PDC1 for essential ethanol production while freeing carbon

overexpressions.rationale: Increases flux from chorismate to prephenate, boosting the precursor supply for phenylalanine biosynthesis which feeds the heterologous pathway
First committed step of shikimate pathway; overexpression of feedback-resistant ARO4(K229L) increases carbon flux into aromatic amino acid biosynthesis
Increases malonyl-CoA supply for the CHS/CHR reaction which requires 3 malonyl-CoA per product molecule; overexpression of feedback-resistant ACC1(S659A,S1157A) recommended

fba_results.strategy_description: Heterologous 5-step pathway (PAL, C4H/CPR, 4CL, CHS+CHR, CHI) from L-phenylalanine to liquiritigenin added to iMM904. Theoretical max flux = 1.075 mmol/gDW/h at 0% biomass, 0.976 mmol/gDW/h at 10% biomass with KO of ARO10/PDC5/PDC6 and OE of ARO7. Production envelope shows linear tradeoff. Key bottlenecks: malonyl-CoA supply (3 mol/product), NADPH (2 mol/product), phenylalanine availability. Supplementary: feedback-resistant ARO4(K229L) and ACC1(S659A,S1157A) mutants recommended.

heterologous_genes.function: UDP-glucosyltransferase that glycosylates tyrosol to produce salidroside using UDP-glucose as sugar donor

knockouts.rationale: Blocks diversion of L-tyrosine to N-formyl-L-tyrosine, preserving tyrosine pool for salidroside biosynthesis
Reduces UDP-glucose consumption by glycogen synthesis, increasing UDP-glucose availability for the UGT73B6 glucosylation step

overexpressions.rationale: First committed step of the shikimate pathway; overexpression with feedback-resistant mutant (ARO4-K229L) increases carbon flux toward aromatic amino acid biosynthesis
Converts prephenate to 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate; overexpression directs more prephenate toward tyrosine branch instead of phenylalanine branch
Increases UDP-glucose supply for the glucosylation step catalyzed by UGT73B6

fba_results.strategy_description: Heterologous pathway (4 steps: TYRTAr, HPPDC, TYROH_ADH, UGT_SAL) added to iMM904 model. Baseline theoretical max is 2.537 mmol/gDW/h at zero growth. With 10% biomass constraint (min growth = 0.0283 h-1), max salidroside flux = 2.293 mmol/gDW/h. Engineering strategy: knockout TYRNFT (YDR403W) to eliminate tyrosine diversion, knockout GSY2 (YLR258W) to free UDP-glucose, overexpress ARO4 (YBR249C) for shikimate flux, overexpress TYR1 (YBR166C) for tyrosine branch, overexpress UGP1 (YKL035W) for UDP-glucose supply. Production envelope shows linear tradeoff between growth and production (2.537 at 0% biomass to 0 at 100% biomass).

knockouts.rationale: Prevents conversion of protoheme (heme B) to heme O, blocking the major product-degrading pathway that diverts heme toward cytochrome c oxidase assembly

overexpressions.rationale: Rate-limiting first step in heme biosynthesis; overexpression increases 5-aminolevulinate (ALA) flux, the committed precursor for the entire pathway

Catalyzes four sequential decarboxylations of uroporphyrinogen III to coproporphyrinogen III; overexpression prevents bottleneck at this branch point where uroporphyrinogen III can be diverted to siroheme
Oxygen-dependent oxidative decarboxylation step; overexpression ensures flux through this step matches upstream ALA supply increase

fba_results.strategy_description: Heme (protoheme/heme B) production in S. cerevisiae using the entirely native heme biosynthesis pathway (KEGG module M00868). All 8 enzymatic steps (HEM1-HEM4, HEM12-HEM15) are present in iMM904. Strategy: (1) KO COX10/YPL172C to block protoheme degradation via heme O synthase; (2) Overexpress HEM1/YDR232W (rate-limiting ALA synthase, min flux 0.5); (3) Overexpress HEM12/YDR047W (uroporphyrinogen decarboxylase, min flux 0.5); (4) Overexpress HEM13/YDR044W (coproporphyrinogen oxidase, min flux 0.5). Added transport (PHEMEtm) and exchange (EX_pheme_c) reactions. Theoretical max 0.5502, optimized 0.5452 at 10% biomass (6% improvement over baseline 0.5141). Aerobic conditions required. Iron supplementation recommended.

heterologous_genes.function: Phenylalanine ammonia-lyase; converts L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamate
Trans-cinnamate 4-monooxygenase; hydroxylates cinnamate to 4-coumarate (CYP450)
NADPH--cytochrome P450 reductase; provides electrons to CYP450 enzymes C4H and IFS
4-coumarate--CoA ligase; activates coumarate to coumaroyl-CoA
Chalcone synthase; condenses coumaroyl-CoA with 3 malonyl-CoA to naringenin chalcone
Chalcone--flavanone isomerase; cyclizes naringenin chalcone to naringenin
2-hydroxyisoflavanone synthase; converts naringenin to 2-hydroxy-2,3-dihydrogenistein (CYP450)
2-hydroxyisoflavanone dehydratase; dehydrates 2-hydroxy-2,3-dihydrogenistein to genistein
Isoflavone 4'-O-methyltransferase; methylates genistein to biochanin A using SAM

overexpressions.rationale: Increase carbon flux into shikimate pathway toward phenylalanine; feedback-resistant ARO4 mutant (K229L) recommended
Increase flux from chorismate to prephenate toward phenylalanine; feedback-resistant ARO7 mutant (T226I) recommended

fba_results.strategy_description: 8-gene heterologous pathway (PAL, C4H, 4CL, CHS, CHI, IFS, HID, IOMT) plus CPR for CYP450 electron transfer from L-phenylalanine to biochanin A. Overexpression of feedback-resistant ARO4 (DAHP synthase) and ARO7 (chorismate mutase) to boost shikimate pathway flux. At 10% minimum biomass, optimized flux = 0.722 mmol/gDW/h vs baseline 0.714 (+1.1%). Theoretical max at zero growth = 0.789 mmol/gDW/h.

heterologous_genes.function: Beta-1,3-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase; transfers GlcNAc from UDP-GlcNAc to lactose forming lacto-N-triose II (GlcNAc-beta1,3-Gal-beta1,4-Glc)
Beta-1,4-galactosyltransferase; transfers Gal from UDP-Gal to lacto-N-triose II forming LNnT (Gal-beta1,4-GlcNAc-beta1,3-Gal-beta1,4-Glc)
Beta-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1; catalyzes lactose synthesis from UDP-galactose and glucose; requires Mn2+ cofactor; PDB structures available (2AE7, 2AEC, etc.)
Lactose permease (587 aa); mediates lactose transport via proton symport; used to facilitate LNnT export or as alternative lactose import route

knockouts.reaction_blocked: GLCS2 (glycogen synthase, UDP-Glc -> glycogen) and GLYGS (glycogen/starch synthase)
knockouts.rationale: Eliminates glycogen synthesis that consumes UDP-glucose (baseline flux 0.149 mmol/gDW/h), redirecting UDP-Glc toward UDP-Gal via GAL10 for LNnT biosynthesis

knockouts.reaction_blocked: UDPGALt2g (UDP-galactose transport from cytoplasm to Golgi)
knockouts.rationale: Prevents loss of cytoplasmic UDP-galactose to the Golgi apparatus, retaining the precursor for the cytoplasmic LNnT pathway which requires 2 UDP-Gal per molecule
Eliminates ergosterol glucosylation that competes for UDP-glucose, further increasing UDP-Glc availability for conversion to UDP-Gal

overexpressions.rationale: Enhances conversion of UDP-glucose to UDP-galactose; two molecules of UDP-Gal are consumed per LNnT (lactose synthesis + final galactosylation); forced min flux = 0.5

overexpressions.reaction_boosted: GF6PTA (glutamine--fructose-6-phosphate transaminase; F6P + Gln -> GlcN-6P + Glu)
overexpressions.rationale: Rate-limiting entry point for UDP-GlcNAc biosynthesis from fructose-6-phosphate; overexpression increases hexosamine pathway flux for UDP-GlcNAc supply; forced min flux = 0.3

overexpressions.reaction_boosted: GALUi (UTP--glucose-1-phosphate uridylyltransferase; G1P + UTP -> UDP-Glc + PPi)
overexpressions.rationale: Increases UDP-glucose supply from glucose-1-phosphate, feeding the GAL10 epimerase for enhanced UDP-Gal production; forced min flux = 0.5

fba_results.strategy_description: Heterologous pathway adds 3 glycosyltransferase reactions (LACS: UDP-Gal + Glc -> Lactose; LGTA: Lactose + UDP-GlcNAc -> LNT-3; LGTB: LNT-3 + UDP-Gal -> LNnT) plus an export reaction. The theoretical ceiling (no growth) is 1.391 mmol/gDW/h on 10 mmol/gDW/h glucose. With 10% minimum biomass constraint and optimized knockouts (GLG1, HUT1, YLR189C) plus overexpressions (GAL10 min=0.5, GFA1 min=0.3, UGP1 min=0.5), the optimized max flux is 1.279 mmol/gDW/h at biomass=0.0237. The production envelope shows a linear tradeoff: at full wild-type growth (0.237), residual LNnT flux is 0.250 mmol/gDW/h. Key metabolic bottleneck is UDP-Gal supply (consumed 2x per LNnT) from the limited UDP-Glc pool. TPS1 and GLG1+GLG2 double knockouts are lethal in the iMM904 model and were avoided.

heterologous_genes.function: Phenylalanine ammonia-lyase; converts L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamate + NH3; first committed step of phenylpropanoid pathway
Trans-cinnamate 4-monooxygenase; hydroxylates trans-cinnamate to p-coumarate; CYP450 requiring heme and CPR
4-Coumarate-CoA ligase; activates p-coumarate to p-coumaroyl-CoA using ATP; Km=38 uM for 4-coumarate
Chalcone synthase; condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with 3 malonyl-CoA to form naringenin chalcone
Chalcone isomerase; cyclizes naringenin chalcone to (2S)-naringenin; well-characterized with crystal structures (PDB: 1EYP)
2-Hydroxyisoflavanone synthase; CYP450 that catalyzes aryl migration converting naringenin to 2-hydroxy-2,3-dihydrogenistein
2-Hydroxyisoflavanone dehydratase; dehydrates 2-hydroxy-2,3-dihydrogenistein to genistein; Km=170 uM
Isoflavone 3'-hydroxylase; CYP450 that hydroxylates genistein at the 3' position to produce 3'-hydroxygenistein (orobol); fungal origin compatible with yeast expression

knockouts.rationale: Blocks degradation of phenylpyruvate (Ehrlich pathway), increasing flux toward phenylalanine biosynthesis via PHETA1 reverse reaction; OR-logic requires combined KO with PDC genes for full effect

overexpressions.rationale: Increases flux from chorismate to prephenate, boosting phenylalanine precursor supply; feedback-resistant mutant ARO7(T226I) preferred
Entry point of pentose phosphate pathway producing NADPH; pathway requires 3 NADPH per product molecule for C4H, IFS, and CYP57B3 reactions
Increases cytoplasmic acetyl-CoA supply which feeds into malonyl-CoA via ACC1; CHS requires 3 malonyl-CoA per naringenin chalcone
Converts acetyl-CoA to malonyl-CoA; overexpression of ACC1(S659A/S1157A) phosphorylation-resistant mutant increases malonyl-CoA pool

fba_results.strategy_description: Heterologous 8-step pathway from L-phenylalanine to 3'-hydroxygenistein via phenylpropanoid, flavonoid, isoflavonoid biosynthesis, and 3'-hydroxylation. Eight plant/fungal enzymes are required (PAL, C4H, 4CL, CHS from A. thaliana; CHI from M. sativa; IFS, HIDH from G. max; CYP57B3 from A. oryzae). Three CYP450 steps (C4H, IFS, CYP57B3) utilize the native yeast NADPH-cytochrome P450 reductase NCP1 (YHR042W). Engineering strategy includes ARO10 knockout to block phenylpyruvate degradation, and overexpression of ARO7 (feedback-resistant mutant), ZWF1, ACS2, and ACC1 to boost precursor and cofactor supply. FBA theoretical maximum flux is 0.667 mmol/gDW/h at zero growth and 0.666 at 10% minimum biomass. Production envelope shows linear growth-production tradeoff. Aerobic conditions required for three O2-dependent CYP450 reactions.

heterologous_genes.function: L-lactate dehydrogenase; converts pyruvate + NADH to L-lactate + NAD+

knockouts.rationale: Major pyruvate decarboxylase; blocks ethanol pathway to redirect pyruvate flux toward lactate (WT flux 15.95)
Isozyme of PDC1; must knock out all three PDC genes due to OR-logic GPR to fully disable PYRDC
Third pyruvate decarboxylase isozyme; required for complete PYRDC elimination
Major alcohol dehydrogenase; blocks acetaldehyde to ethanol conversion (WT flux 15.82)
Alcohol dehydrogenase isozyme; OR-logic GPR requires multi-gene knockout
Alcohol dehydrogenase isozyme; completes elimination of ethanol production
Degrades L-lactate back to pyruvate; knockout prevents product degradation
Blocks glycerol pathway to redirect NADH toward LDH reaction (WT flux 0.007)

overexpressions.rationale: Increase pyruvate supply from PEP; ensures high substrate availability for LDH

fba_results.strategy_description: Express heterologous L-LDH from L. casei (P00343). Knock out all three pyruvate decarboxylase genes (PDC1/PDC5/PDC6), three alcohol dehydrogenase genes (ADH1/ADH3/ADH5), CYB2 (lactate degradation), and GPD1 (glycerol pathway). This eliminates ethanol and glycerol as competing pathways, prevents L-lactate degradation, and redirects nearly all glycolytic flux through LDH. With 10% minimum biomass constraint, achieves 19.65 mmol/gDW/h lactate production (98.2% of theoretical maximum 20.0). Production envelope shows linear tradeoff: at full biomass (0.287) production is 15.83; at zero biomass production reaches 20.0.
